name: Drop-Through Platform
description: An intentional input that lets the player fall through an eligible platform.
design_notes: Drop-through is an input and collision interaction layered on top of one-way platforms. It should prevent accidental drops while staying fast enough for combat and traversal.
design_purpose: Let players move downward through layered platform spaces.
Support vertical arenas and route choice.
Make platform stacks navigable without ladders or gaps.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can intentionally move down through layered platforms without fighting collision.

parameters.name: drop_input_chord
parameters.description: Input combination required to start the drop.

parameters.name: drop_collision_disable_ms
parameters.description: Window where the current platform ignores the player.

parameters.name: minimum_platform_clearance
parameters.description: Distance the player must clear before collision can return.

edge_cases: Drop input while crouched, attacking, or stunned should not accidentally fire.
Dropping through a moving platform should preserve expected relative motion.
Stacked platforms need a rule for whether one input drops through one or many layers.
common_bugs: Collision is disabled for all platforms instead of only the current platform.
The player immediately lands again because collision returns before clearing thickness.
Input buffering turns a normal landing jump into an unintended drop.
balancing_notes: Fast drop-through supports arena flow; strict input chords reduce accidental falls in precision layouts.
Hazards beneath platforms should be telegraphed if drop-through is common.
accessibility_notes: Allow remapping the drop input and avoid requiring awkward simultaneous inputs on small controllers.

implementation_notes.unity: On a valid drop input chord, add the current platform collider to an actor-local ignore set for drop_collision_disable_ms and clear it only after the actor bottom passes below the platform.
implementation_notes.godot: Switch collision masks or one-way platform ignore state for the specific body after down+jump or down+interact, then restore collision once clearance is reached.
implementation_notes.unreal: Track the platform being stood on and apply a temporary movement ignore rule or custom floor rejection for that actor rather than disabling collision for every player.
implementation_notes.web: Store ignored_platform_id and drop_until_ms on the actor, skip only that platform during vertical collision resolution, and re-enable collision after minimum_platform_clearance.

example_games.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate
example_games.note: Uses platform dropping as a common arena movement action.
